summary:
The apical migration of the epithelial attachment causes a loss of clinical attachment and ultimately leads to the formation of periodontal pockets. This process is central to the progression of chronic periodontitis due to increased probing depths beyond normal physiological limits.

The apical migration of the epithelial attachment results in loss of clinical attachment and forms periodontal pockets. Periodontal pocket formation is a direct consequence of this pathological shift and marks a critical step in disease progression within periodontitis. 

Only include information present in the original text; do not add external knowledge or make assumptions.
